assertions.description: navigator.gpu is the entry point for WebGPU; primitive_index rides on this surface.
navigator.gpu.requestAdapter is a function — the surface used to inspect which optional features (including primitive-index) the device supports.
GPUAdapter exposes a `features` GPUSupportedFeatures set, which is where the 'primitive-index' feature string appears when supported.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu)return false;const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();return !!(a && a.features && typeof a.features.has==='function');})()

assertions.description: Userland can call features.has('primitive-index'); calling has() returns a boolean without throwing whether or not the feature is present.
Requesting a device with requiredFeatures=['primitive-index'] from an adapter that does not list it rejects.

GPUShaderModule descriptor accepts a WGSL string referencing @builtin(primitive_index); device.createShaderModule should accept the source even if compilation later errors when the feature is missing.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu)return false;const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();if(!a)return false;const d=await a.requestDevice();return typeof d.createShaderModule==='function';})()
